name: Licensing Information
classification_notes: No typeface was present on this page; this is a licensing information page. Category fields are placeholder values only.
summary: This page contains licensing information for Facetype's typefaces, not a specific typeface entry.
description: The page describes Facetype's licensing philosophy, which prices font licences according to the size of the company obtaining the licence rather than the number of workstations. Facetype works with Font Radar to monitor legitimate usage of their typefaces. No specific typeface is featured on this page.
foundry_blurb: Facetype explain that their licence fees are calculated based on the size of the client company rather than workstation count, reflecting the commercial value a company derives from using their typefaces. They collaborate with Font Radar to ensure legitimate usage and invite enquiries directly.

name: Facetype In Use
classification_notes: The page is a gallery of typeface usage examples and does not represent a single typeface; no classification is possible.
summary: A gallery page showing real-world usage of Facetype typefaces across print, digital and motion contexts.
description: This page collects examples of Facetype typefaces deployed in published work, including editorial, branding and streaming projects. Featured typefaces include Strangelove Next, Publica Sans, Ivory, Plaquette, Doll and Slug. It serves as a specimen gallery rather than a page dedicated to any single typeface.
foundry_blurb: The Facetype 'In Use' page gathers real-world examples of their typefaces appearing in commercial and creative projects, spanning clients such as Netflix, Disney Pixar and Adbusters.
